questions.question: What is the primary purpose of Notion Academy?

options.explanation: Selling templates is a feature of the Marketplace, not the core purpose of the Academy.

options.text: To provide a place to earn badges, learn Notion, and get certified
options.explanation: The Academy’s main goal is to help users learn Notion, earn badges, and become certified.
Webinars are offered, but they are just one component of a broader learning platform.

options.text: To replace the Notion Help Center
options.explanation: The Help Center remains a separate resource for quick answers.

questions.question: Which badge‑related statement is true according to the article?

options.text: Badges are only available to Enterprise plan users
options.explanation: The article says badges can be earned at any plan type.

options.text: You earn badges by completing a learning path
options.explanation: Paths are curated learning tracks that, when completed, grant badges.

options.text: Badges are awarded automatically when you create a new page
options.explanation: Badges are tied to learning activities, not page creation.
Badges and certifications are separate recognitions.

questions.question: Which feature is included in the Notion Certified Admin program?

options.explanation: The program teaches admins how to run Notion at scale.

options.text: Learning how to code custom widgets
options.explanation: Coding custom widgets is not mentioned as part of the certification.
Marketing flyer design is unrelated to admin certification.
The focus is on enterprise‑level administration, not personal reminders.

questions.question: Which lesson is labeled "Intermediate" and teaches how to build an intake system using forms, charts, and automations?

options.explanation: Custom Agents is a beginner‑level lesson about automation, not the intake‑system lesson.

options.text: An issue tracker for any team
options.explanation: The description matches the intake‑system lesson listed as intermediate.
Workspace design is an advanced lesson focused on layout, not forms and charts.
Templates & marketplace is an intermediate lesson about template business, not intake systems.

questions.question: How many individual lessons are included in the "Essentials Path"?

options.explanation: The article lists "3 Lessons" for the Essentials Path.
Four lessons belong to the Workflows Path, not Essentials.
Five lessons are in the Advanced Path.
No path in the article contains six lessons.

questions.question: Which learning path is described as helping users "Create connected systems that unite teams and projects, with the help of AI"?

options.explanation: Essentials Path focuses on basic skills, not AI‑enabled connected systems.
The description matches the Workflows Path.
Advanced Path covers automation and architecture but does not mention AI in its brief description.
Custom Agents is a single lesson, not a multi‑lesson path.

questions.question: Which lesson listed in the article has the longest duration?

options.explanation: At 40 minutes, this lesson is longer than any other listed.
Project management is shorter than the 40‑minute template lesson.
Workspace design is even shorter.
While longer than some, it is still shorter than 40 minutes.

questions.question: Which of the following category labels is NOT explicitly used for any lesson in the article?

options.explanation: Essentials appears as a category for several lessons.
Workflows is used for multiple lessons.
AI is mentioned in a path description but not as a lesson category label.
Badged Path is listed as a type of learning path.

questions.question: A learner who wants to focus on automating work and learning workspace‑architecture best practices should enroll in which path, and what is its difficulty level?

options.explanation: The Advanced Path is described as covering automation, architecture, and complex capabilities, and its difficulty is listed as Advanced.
Workflows focuses on connected systems with AI, not specifically on deep automation and architecture.
Essentials provides basic skills, insufficient for advanced automation.
Custom Agents is a single, beginner‑level lesson, not a full path.

questions.question: Based on the article’s layout, which statement best describes the relationship between “Badged Path” and “Lesson” labels?

options.text: All Badged Paths consist solely of Lessons.
options.explanation: While Badged Paths contain lessons, the article does not state they consist *solely* of lessons; they are broader learning tracks.

options.text: A Badged Path is a type of learning track that may contain multiple Lessons.
options.explanation: The article lists Badged Paths (e.g., Essentials, Workflows, Advanced) each described as containing several lessons.

options.text: Lesson is a higher‑level grouping that contains Badged Paths.
options.explanation: Lessons are individual units; Badged Paths are collections of lessons, not the other way around.

options.text: Badged Path and Lesson are interchangeable terms.
options.explanation: The two terms refer to different levels of granularity and are not interchangeable.
